content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

October 1, 1976

Mr. Forest Greber
Deputy County Counsel
County of Kern
1415 Truxtun Avenue
Bakersfield, CA 93301

Dear Mr. Greber:

You ask whether a number of "advisory committees" including
the following:

Kern County Water Resources Committee,
Mental Health Advisory Committee,
Technical Advisory Committee on Drug Abuse,
Alcoholism Advisory Committee,
Emergency Medical Care Committee,
Advisory Board for Child Health and Disability Prevention
Program,
Grazing Advisory Board (Section 15),
Grazing Advisory Committee (Section 3), and
Kern County Parks and Recreation Commission,

may be required by the Board of Supervisors of Kern County to
develop Conflict of Interest Codes pursuant to Government Code
Section 87300. You indicate that these committees are solely
advisory within the meaning of Government Code Section 82019 in
that they possess no decision-making authority as indicated in the
definition of "member in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(1). As
you may be aware, this regulation of the Commission has been
amended, copy attached.

If the Committees and Commissions in question are not decision-
making bodies within the meaning of the regulation, their function
may be classified as solely advisory within the meaning of Government Code Section 82019. Thus, if the members of such Committees
and Commissions are uncompensated, they clearly fall within the
exemption contained in Government Code Section 82019.

While it may be said that members of boards or commissions
which are solely advisory in nature may participate in the making
of a governmental decision within the meaning of 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Section 18700(c), if the function of their board is solely
advisory, the members of such a board or commission are still

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 · EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 · ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

-2-

exempt by virtue of Government Code Section 82019.

If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to contact
me at (916)322-6444.

Sincerely,


Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

DLS:mra

Attachment

OFFICE OF THE

COUNTY COUNSEL

Room 702
Administration and Courts Building
Telephone (805) 861-2326

COUNTY OF KERN
1415 Truxton Avenue
Bakersfield, California
93301

RALPH B. JORDAN
County Counsel

September 14, 1976

In reply please refer
to Our File Number:

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Conflicts of Interest Division
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Advisory Committees

Gentlemen:

There are in Kern County a number of advisory committees,
including the following:

Kern County Water Resources Committee

Mental Health Advisory Committee

Technical Advisory Committee on Drug Abuse

Alcoholism Advisory Committee

Emergency Medical Care Committee

Advisory Board for Child Health and
Disability Prevention Program

Grazing Advisory Board (Section 15)

Grazing Advisory Committee (Section 3)

Kern County Parks and Recreation Commission

These committees have been appointed by the Board of Supervisors,
and we are advised that their function is "solely advisory". (Government
Code Section 82019.)

We are further advised that these committees possess no "decision-
making authority" as that term is defined in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700
in that:

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Conflicts of Interest Division
September 14, 1976
Page Two

(A)  They make no final governmental decisions;

(B)  They may not compel a governmental decision
by any agency;

(C)  Their actions, recommendations, or considera-
tions are not a legal prerequisite to a final
governmental decision; or

(D)  They do not make substantive recommendations
which are, and over an extended period of
time have been, regularly approved without
significant amendment or modification by
another public official or governmental
agency.

Are these committees required to adopt conflict of interest codes?

Is the definition of "participates in the making of a governmental
decision" material in the resolution of the question? (2 Cal. Adm. Code
Section 18700(c).)

The deadline for the filing of conflict of interest codes has
been set by the Board of Supervisors as October 29, 1976, and therefore,
we would appreciate your views on this question as soon as possible.

Thank you for your courtesy and consideration.

Yours very truly,

RALPH B. JORDAN, County Counsel

By [signature]
Forest Greber
Deputy County Counsel

FG:b

sections.question: You ask whether a number of "advisory committees" including the following: Kern County Water Resources Committee, Mental Health Advisory Committee, Technical Advisory Committee on Drug Abuse, Alcoholism Advisory Committee, Emergency Medical Care Committee, Advisory Board for Child Health and Disability Prevention Program, Grazing Advisory Board (Section 15), Grazing Advisory Committee (Section 3), and Kern County Parks and Recreation Commission, may be required by the Board of Supervisors of Kern County to develop Conflict of Interest Codes pursuant to Government Code Section 87300.
sections.conclusion: If the Committees and Commissions in question are not decision-making bodies within the meaning of the regulation, their function may be classified as solely advisory within the meaning of Government Code Section 82019. Thus, if the members of such Committees and Commissions are uncompensated, they clearly fall within the exemption contained in Government Code Section 82019. While it may be said that members of boards or commissions which are solely advisory in nature may participate in the making of a governmental decision within the meaning of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(c), if the function of their board is solely advisory, the members of such a board or commission are still exempt by virtue of Government Code Section 82019.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether various Kern County advisory committees that are solely advisory in nature and possess no decision-making authority are required by the Board of Supervisors to develop Conflict of Interest Codes under Government Code Section 87300.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Solely advisory committees with uncompensated members are exempt from Conflict of Interest Code requirements under Government Code Section 82019, even if their members may participate in governmental decision-making, because their function is purely advisory rather than decision-making.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an FPPC response letter dated October 1, 1976. The incoming request from Kern County is appended to the response, providing full context. Question and conclusion sections extracted directly from letter text. Minor confidence reduction due to letter being primarily composed as flowing prose rather than discrete sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You ask whether a number of "advisory committees" including the following: Kern County Water Resources Committee, Mental Health Advisory Committee, Technical Advisory Committee on Drug Abuse, Alcoholism Advisory Committee, Emergency Medical Care Committee, Advisory Board for Child Health and Disability Prevention Program, Grazing Advisory Board (Section 15), Grazing Advisory Committee (Section 3), and Kern County Parks and Recreation Commission, may be required by the Board of Supervisors of Kern County to develop Conflict of Interest Codes pursuant to Government Code Section 87300.

CONCLUSION: If the Committees and Commissions in question are not decision-making bodies within the meaning of the regulation, their function may be classified as solely advisory within the meaning of Government Code Section 82019. Thus, if the members of such Committees and Commissions are uncompensated, they clearly fall within the exemption contained in Government Code Section 82019. While it may be said that members of boards or commissions which are solely advisory in nature may participate in the making of a governmental decision within the meaning of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(c), if the function of their board is solely advisory, the members of such a board or commission are still exempt by virtue of Government Code Section 82019.

You indicate that these committees are solely advisory within the meaning of Government Code Section 82019 in that they possess no decision-making authority as indicated in the definition of "member in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(1). As you may be aware, this regulation of the Commission has been amended, copy attached. Spurlock, Jr., Chief Conflicts of Interest Division DLS:mra Attachment This is a letter from the Office of the County Counsel for the County of Kern, dated September 14, 1976. The letter is addressed to the Fair Political Practices Commission, Conflicts of Interest Division, in Sacramento, California. The letter discusses advisory committees in Kern County and provides information about their composition and function. The letter states that there are several advisory committees in Kern County, including: - Kern County Water Resources Committee - Mental Health Advisory Committee - Technical Advisory Committee on Drug Abuse - Alcoholism Advisory Committee - Emergency Medical Care Committee - Advisory Board for Child Health and Disability Prevention Program - Grazing Advisory Board (Section 15) - Grazing Advisory Committee (Section 3) - Kern County Parks and Recreation Commission These committees have been appointed by the Board of Supervisors, and their function is described as "solely advisory" (Government Code Section 82019). The letter also notes that these committees possess no "decision-making authority" as defined in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700. (A) They make no final governmental decisions; (B) They may not compel a governmental decision by any agency; (C) Their actions, recommendations, or considerations are not a legal prerequisite to a final governmental decision; or
embedding.summary: FPPC Chief Delbert Spurlock advises Kern County Deputy Counsel Forest Greber that numerous Kern County advisory committees are exempt from developing Conflict of Interest Codes because they are solely advisory bodies with uncompensated members under Government Code Section 82019.
